jokes.text: You might want to change them pants.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil trying to impress an important client
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt fashion critique undermining Phil's confidence
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will moment - direct, unexpected fashion advice that deflates Phil's preparation. Great character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Maybe he can pick one out of Uncle Phil's pants.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Jameson's bad luck color being red
jokes.punchline: Will connecting it back to Phil's questionable pants
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that builds on the earlier pants joke, showing Will's persistence with the insult.

jokes.text: Carlton, what is it, recess at the clown convention?
jokes.setup: Carlton appearing in presumably colorful golf attire
jokes.punchline: Will's elaborate metaphor for Carlton's outfit
jokes.explanation: Creative insult with strong imagery. The 'clown convention' escalation makes it memorable.

jokes.text: That's right. And by the way, Will, who cut your hair, Ray Charles?
jokes.setup: Carlton responding to Will's insult about his outfit
jokes.punchline: Comeback suggesting Will's barber was blind
jokes.explanation: Solid comeback from Carlton, though the Ray Charles reference is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: I've been saving that one for a week. Don't even try to top it.
jokes.setup: Carlton's proud delivery of his comeback
jokes.punchline: Meta commentary on his own joke preparation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - earnest pride in his joke-crafting reveals his character beautifully.

jokes.text: Back in Philly I knew this guy named Leo Brentley. The dude tried to steal my lucky drawers.
jokes.setup: Jameson mentioning not trusting Leos
jokes.punchline: Will's completely inappropriate and absurd Philadelphia story
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Will moment - takes innocent conversation into wonderfully inappropriate territory. 'Lucky drawers' is quotable gold.

jokes.text: You have a pair of lucky drawers? - Well, yeah. - So do I.
jokes.setup: Will mentions lucky underwear
jokes.punchline: Both Will and wealthy businessman Jameson have lucky underwear
jokes.explanation: Unexpected common ground between different characters - absurd but relatable

jokes.text: This is all very interesting.
jokes.setup: The bizarre underwear conversation between Will and Carlton
jokes.punchline: Jameson's polite but strained attempt to process the weirdness
jokes.explanation: Good straight-man reaction that highlights the absurdity of the underwear discussion.

jokes.text: I ain't down with carrying golf clubs. / It's $50, plus tip. / Fore!
jokes.setup: Will refusing to caddy
jokes.punchline: Immediate attitude change at mention of money
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with excellent economy. Will's principles vs. money creates instant character comedy.

jokes.text: It's because your hat's turned to the side, you can see better.
jokes.setup: Jameson making a miraculous hole-in-one shot
jokes.punchline: Will's completely illogical explanation for the success
jokes.explanation: Classic Will logic - confidently wrong but delivered with such certainty it's funny.

jokes.text: That's wack. / 'Wack.' In what sense of the word?
jokes.setup: Will using slang to describe Jameson's work obsession
jokes.punchline: Jameson's formal, confused response to street slang
jokes.explanation: Nice culture clash moment showing the generational/class divide between characters.

jokes.text: And was your sucking up as successful as you'd hoped?
jokes.setup: Phil proudly talking about impressing Jameson
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's blunt assessment of Phil's behavior
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey moment - cutting through Phil's pride with British directness.

jokes.text: But, Carlton, Dad's his lawyer. Isn't that kind of using a connection? / I can't believe it, Vivian.
jokes.setup: Ashley pointing out Carlton's nepotism
jokes.punchline: Phil completely ignoring the valid point to focus on golf
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phil's selective hearing when it comes to ethics vs. golf.

jokes.text: What's his handicap, sir? / Ain't it obvious?
jokes.setup: Geoffrey asking about Jameson's golf handicap
jokes.punchline: Will interpreting it as mental handicap
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect setup and economy. Will's interpretation is both obvious and unexpected.

jokes.text: I just told my boss, 'Teach me. I'm a blank slate.' / At least you're honest.
jokes.setup: Hilary earnestly describing her learning attitude
jokes.punchline: Will's cutting observation about her intellect
jokes.explanation: Good Hilary/Will dynamic - her oblivious setup allows for his perfect putdown.

jokes.text: So, when customers come in the door, I ignore them. If they ask me about a piece of art, I look right through them. If they ask for a price, I go like this... [gesture] And walk away.
jokes.setup: Describing her job at an exclusive art gallery
jokes.punchline: Her job is literally to be rude and snobbish
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - she's naturally qualified for a job requiring snobbery. The escalating rudeness is well-structured.

jokes.text: Hilary, it sounds like they hired you to be a snob. / I guess they saw something in me.
jokes.setup: Ashley pointing out the obvious about Hilary's job
jokes.punchline: Hilary's oblivious pride in her natural snobbery
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character comedy - Hilary's complete lack of self-awareness makes this land perfectly.

jokes.text: No, sir, my name is Carlton.
jokes.setup: Jameson clearly asking for Will
jokes.punchline: Carlton's hopeful but wrong assumption
jokes.explanation: Good Carlton moment showing his eagerness to be important, even when he's clearly not wanted.

jokes.text: It's a sprig of wolfsbane. Hang it over your kitchen door and all will be well. / But Jameson, everything's fine in our kitchen. / That you know of.
jokes.setup: Jameson's strange superstitious gift
jokes.punchline: His ominous implication about unknown kitchen dangers
jokes.explanation: Great character moment establishing Jameson's paranoid worldview with perfect ominous delivery.

jokes.text: I would've lost millions, but thanks to my 'Yo, homey' here...
jokes.setup: Wealthy white businessman trying to use street slang
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt at Will's vernacular
jokes.explanation: Good culture clash humor - Jameson's awkward appropriation of Will's slang.

jokes.text: So what you're saying is thanks to me, you got lucky twice yesterday. / Three times.
jokes.setup: Will counting Jameson's lucky breaks
jokes.punchline: Jameson's implication about marital relations
jokes.explanation: Solid innuendo joke with great economy, though the setup is somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: The wife girlie will be smiling for months.
jokes.setup: Following up on Jameson's marital luck comment
jokes.punchline: Will's street-flavored confirmation of the innuendo
jokes.explanation: Follow-up to the innuendo with Will's characteristic phrasing, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: I don't drink. I like to keep my mind clear. / I think he's cleared it out pretty well.
jokes.setup: Jameson explaining his sobriety
jokes.punchline: Will's interpretation that his mind is actually empty
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect timing. The 'cleared out' twist on 'clear mind' is cleverly constructed.

jokes.text: I was born August 4, 1974. - A Leo?
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions his birthday
jokes.punchline: Jameson's horrified reaction to Carlton being a Leo
jokes.explanation: Callbacks to earlier Leo distrust, Carlton inadvertently makes himself the enemy

jokes.text: Why is that man running loose on the streets without medication?
jokes.setup: Jameson's increasingly bizarre behavior
jokes.punchline: Vivian's medical assessment of his mental state
jokes.explanation: Perfect Vivian reaction - direct, medical perspective on the absurdity. Great economy and timing.

jokes.text: Last year... Jameson was taking all of his stock advice from his dog. / That's untrue, Vivian. He only took tax advice from Sparky.
jokes.setup: Vivian explaining Jameson's dog advisor
jokes.punchline: Phil's 'correction' that makes it even more absurd
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Phil's 'correction' makes the situation even more ridiculous while maintaining his lawyer's precision.

jokes.text: He talks to the spirit of a dead dog? / No. He had him stuffed and put in his office.
jokes.setup: Will's horrified question about supernatural dog communication
jokes.punchline: Vivian's revelation that it's even weirder - taxidermy consultation
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that subverts expectations - the reality is somehow more disturbing than the supernatural alternative.

jokes.text: The planet he's on ain't even been discovered yet.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Jameson's bizarre behavior
jokes.punchline: Will's creative metaphor for extreme mental distance
jokes.explanation: Great Will metaphor with strong quotability. The 'undiscovered planet' concept perfectly captures Jameson's mental state.

jokes.text: Exactly. That's Sparky's job.
jokes.setup: Phil saying Will shouldn't give financial advice
jokes.punchline: Will's deadpan acknowledgment that the dead stuffed dog should handle it
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback with great economy. Will's matter-of-fact acceptance of the absurd situation.

jokes.text: Federal taxes? This is an outrage. Didn't President Bush say, 'No new taxes'? / But federal taxes aren't new. / They are to me.
jokes.setup: Hilary discovering taxes on her first paycheck
jokes.punchline: Her literal interpretation of 'new taxes' applying to her personal experience
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary logic - her self-centered worldview makes 'new taxes' purely about her experience. Great character integration.

jokes.text: And who is this FICA guy?
jokes.setup: Hilary examining her paycheck deductions
jokes.punchline: Thinking FICA is a person's name rather than an acronym
jokes.explanation: Classic Hilary misunderstanding - personalizing an impersonal system perfectly fits her character.

jokes.text: That may seem like a lot, Will, but wait till they take out all the taxes.
jokes.setup: Will mentioning making Jameson $1 million
jokes.punchline: Carlton applying Hilary's tax trauma to Will's situation
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the tax revelation, though not as strong as the original moment.

jokes.text: Size 42, extra fly.
jokes.setup: Describing his custom suit measurements
jokes.punchline: Using 'fly' (cool/stylish) instead of 'long' for suit measurements
jokes.explanation: Great Will wordplay mixing fashion terminology with slang. 'Extra fly' is quotable and character-perfect.

jokes.text: Does he need a butler? Just asking. For a friend.
jokes.setup: Will mentions fringe benefits at his job
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey's transparent interest in the position
jokes.explanation: Good Geoffrey moment with his dry delivery and transparent excuse

jokes.text: I know. I just feel terrible. Am I showing enough cuff?
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil says Will is in deeper trouble
jokes.punchline: Will's response shows he's enjoying the lifestyle, not feeling bad
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Will's priorities - fashion over responsibility

jokes.text: Philip, why don't you tell Will about Sparky? / I don't think we need to go into that. / Last year... Jameson was taking all of his stock advice from his dog.
jokes.setup: Phil's reluctance to explain Jameson's dog advisor
jokes.punchline: The uncomfortable revelation of the truth
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Phil's professional embarrassment coming through clearly.

jokes.text: I thought we had agreed on groveling.
jokes.setup: The lawyers need a strategy to win back Jameson
jokes.punchline: Henry's matter-of-fact reference to their planned humiliation
jokes.explanation: Good professional desperation humor - treating groveling as business strategy

jokes.text: I'm sorry, but the aura is wack.
jokes.setup: Will adopting Jameson's mystical language
jokes.punchline: Mixing new age spirituality with street slang
jokes.explanation: Perfect blend of Will's vernacular with mystical concepts. 'Aura is wack' is highly quotable.

jokes.text: What did you say, Sparky? He's talking? He hasn't talked in weeks.
jokes.setup: Jameson consulting his stuffed dog
jokes.punchline: Acting like the dead dog's silence was unusual rather than expected
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - treating the dog's death-silence as temporary rather than permanent. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: It's two against one.
jokes.setup: Sparky 'agreeing' to have the meeting
jokes.punchline: Will treating the human-stuffed dog vote count as legitimate democracy
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and absurdist logic. Will's matter-of-fact vote counting includes the dead dog.

jokes.text: Great. That's your lucky number.
jokes.setup: Stock dropping to $3, Jameson losing money
jokes.punchline: Carlton's sarcastic reference to the lucky number 3
jokes.explanation: Good callback with perfect ironic timing as the 'lucky' number brings disaster.

jokes.text: Will, you owe me $14 million. / Here's $5.
jokes.setup: Massive financial loss demand
jokes.punchline: Will's hilariously inadequate payment offer
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection with incredible economy. The scale mismatch between $14 million and $5 is brilliant.

jokes.text: You're the one that's got Lassie as your vice president.
jokes.setup: Jameson calling Will crazy
jokes.punchline: Will's comeback referencing the famous TV dog
jokes.explanation: Good pop culture callback to Lassie with solid structure, though not particularly original.

jokes.text: Stuck in the office buried under paperwork... because Sparky chased a Buick... and you thought that was his way of telling you to take over General Motors.
jokes.setup: Phil's frustration with Jameson's dog-based decisions
jokes.punchline: The absurd logic chain from dog chasing car to corporate takeover
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation showing the absurd logical leaps Jameson makes. The specificity of 'chased a Buick = buy GM' is brilliant.

jokes.text: Philip, you're just the goat I've been looking for.
jokes.setup: Jameson discovering Phil is a Capricorn
jokes.punchline: Referring to him as the literal zodiac symbol (goat)
jokes.explanation: Solid zodiac wordplay though somewhat predictable. The 'goat' reference works for Capricorn.

jokes.text: Uncle Phil told him he could take his big account... fold it five ways and stick it where Sparky can't find it.
jokes.setup: Will describing Phil's confrontation with Jameson
jokes.punchline: Creative euphemism incorporating the dead dog
jokes.explanation: Creative euphemism that perfectly incorporates the Sparky element. 'Where Sparky can't find it' is brilliantly quotable.

jokes.text: You know, I've always wished I had the nerve... to tell off my employers.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey commenting on Phil standing up to Jameson
jokes.punchline: His transparent desire to tell off his current employers (the Banks family)
jokes.explanation: Good Geoffrey character moment - his subtle rebellion against the family

jokes.text: Capricorn? Philip, your birthday's January 30. You're an Aquarius. / Vivian, for a $20 million account, I'm a Capricorn.
jokes.setup: Vivian correcting Phil's zodiac sign
jokes.punchline: Phil's pragmatic willingness to lie about his identity for money
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode closer showing Phil's pragmatic corruption. The line 'for a $20 million account, I'm a Capricorn' is brilliantly quotable and captures his character arc.
